hero_subtitle: SFTY streamlines identity and access management with role-based controls and audit-ready governance to meet institutional trust and compliance requirements for digital asset businesses.
trust_card_1_value: Identity checks connected to onboarding decisions
trust_card_2_value: Corporate verification, UBO review and entity risk
trust_card_3_value: Wallet and transaction monitoring in one workflow
trust_card_4_value: Message routing, completeness checks and exception handling
trust_card_5_value: Regulatory changes mapped to internal rule
trust_card_6_value: Decision logs and evidence for every review
problem_title: Identity and access controls remain disconnected
problem_description: Compliance teams struggle to unify identity and access across fragmented systems, creating gaps in governance and audit trails. This disconnect leads to inconsistent permissions, weak evidence integrity, and challenges in meeting institutional trust and regulatory expectations.
problem_point_1_value: KYC, KYB, KYT and Travel Rule outputs sit in separate tools
problem_point_2_value: Teams move data between dashboards, spreadsheets and internal notes
problem_point_3_value: Regulatory changes are hard to map into internal workflows
problem_point_4_value: Evidence and decision history are difficult to reconstruct later
solution_description: SFTY connects enterprise identity systems and role-based permissions into one auditable control layer that enforces consistent access, preserves evidence integrity, and supports institutional compliance requirements for VASPs.
workflow_title: From identity access to audit evidence
workflow_description: SFTY integrates identity and access data with compliance roles and permissions to preserve audit evidence and enforce secure governance workflows.
workflow_step_1_description: KYC, KYB, KYT and screening outputs enter SFTY
workflow_step_2_description: Risk rules route checks into the right workflow
workflow_step_3_description: A case opens with customer, wallet and provider context
workflow_step_4_description: Teams review, decide and record the outcome
workflow_step_5_description: Logs, decisions and reports stay ready for audit
why_sfty_title: Not another identity tool. The access control layer
why_sfty_description: SFTY connects identity and access systems with compliance roles, preserving audit-ready evidence and supporting governance for regulated digital asset businesses.
why_sfty_card_1_description: Work above existing KYC, KYB, KYT and Travel Rule tools
why_sfty_card_2_description: Turn disconnected outputs into structured reviews and cases
why_sfty_card_3_description: Preserve evidence, reviewer actions and decision history
why_sfty_card_4_description: Give compliance, risk and leadership one operating view
authority_description: SFTY supports VASPs by integrating identity and access systems with role-based permissions, audit evidence, bank due diligence and secure enterprise deployment workflows.
authority_card_1_description: Connect KYC, KYB, KYT, Travel Rule and screening providers into one workflow
authority_card_2_description: Preserve provider outputs, reviewer notes, decisions and supporting records
authority_card_3_description: Prepare clearer compliance evidence for banks, partners and external review
authority_card_4_description: Support private environments, permissions, integrations and internal governance
ask_sfty_placeholder: How can SFTY integrate identity with compliance access roles
ask_sfty_chip_1_message: Explain how identity access controls support audit readiness
ask_sfty_chip_2_message: Show how SFTY manages role-based permissions for VASPs
ask_sfty_chip_3_message: Compare enterprise deployment options for identity and governance
ask_sfty_helper_text: AI assistant for product, deployment and compliance questions
faq_description: Clear answers on provider orchestration, Travel Rule workflows, audit evidence and enterprise compliance operations.
faq_1_question: How does SFTY integrate with identity systems?
faq_1_answer: SFTY connects existing identity and access systems with its compliance roles, enabling seamless governance and permissions within regulated digital asset workflows.
faq_2_question: How does SFTY manage role-based access control?
faq_2_answer: SFTY supports granular role-based access control, allowing teams to assign permissions and segregate duties across case reviews, decisions, and reporting activities.
faq_3_question: How is audit-ready evidence preserved in SFTY?
faq_3_answer: SFTY preserves audit logs, decision histories, and access records to support traceability and support compliance inspections or internal reviews.
faq_4_question: Can SFTY support enterprise deployment security?
faq_4_answer: SFTY supports controlled enterprise deployments with configurable access governance, audit logging, encryption, and compliance-focused security measures tailored for regulated environments.
faq_5_question: Does SFTY replace existing access or identity providers?
faq_5_answer: No. SFTY sits above identity and access providers, enhancing governance without replacing existing identity management or authentication systems.
final_title: Review identity and access governance with SFTY
final_description: Explore how SFTY integrates identity, access control and audit logs to support enterprise compliance workflows for VASPs.
footer_description: Supervisory and compliance control layer for digital asset markets, businesses and regulated institutions.

footer_legal_notice: This page is for informational purposes only and does not constitute legal, regulatory or compliance advice. SFTY deployment depends on jurisdiction, client requirements and applicable laws.
